model_narrative: We plan the capacity expansion of a single facility over a sequence of time periods, facing uncertain future demand described by a collection of scenarios. In each period we decide how many units of each available component to install, where every installation is a whole number of modules that permanently raise the facility's capacity. We also track the installed capacity reached in each period and the worst capacity shortfall that occurs under each demand scenario. Installation costs are discounted to reflect when they are incurred, and any capacity shortfall is charged a penalty. The objective is to minimize the total discounted installation cost plus the expected shortage penalty across the scenarios.

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the set of available expansion components, each a discrete capacity module that can be installed

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the planning time periods in chronological order; the first member is the opening period and each later member immediately follows the one before it

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the set of demand scenarios, each a possible realization of future demand used to make the plan robust

params.name: c
params.doc: the capacity that one unit of each component contributes once installed, in capacity units per unit installed

params.name: p
params.doc: the purchase cost of one unit of each component, in dollars per unit installed

params.name: dis
params.doc: the discount factor applied to costs incurred in each period, a multiplier between zero and one that shrinks for later periods

params.name: D
params.doc: the demand that must be met in each period under each scenario, in capacity units

params.name: w
params.doc: the shortage penalty charged per unit of unmet demand, a scalar in dollars per capacity unit

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the whole number of units of each component installed in each period; installations are permanent and add to capacity from that period onward

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the worst capacity shortfall experienced under each scenario across the horizon, in capacity units

vars.name: cap
vars.doc: the total installed capacity available in each period

vars.name: obj_var
vars.doc: the total discounted installation cost plus the expected shortage penalty, in dollars

constraints.name: t.prev(t)
constraints.intent: The installed capacity available in each period must reflect everything installed so far. For each period, the capacity in that period equals the capacity carried over from the immediately preceding period plus the capacity added by all components installed in the current period, where a component's contribution is its number of units installed times the capacity each unit provides. In the very first period there is no preceding period, so the capacity comes entirely from what is installed in that opening period.

constraints.name: cap[t]
constraints.intent: In every period and under every demand scenario, the demand must be covered by the installed capacity together with whatever shortfall is absorbed for that scenario. For each period and scenario, the installed capacity in that period plus the shortfall recorded for that scenario must be at least the demand for that period and scenario.

constraints.name: s)
constraints.intent: The total cost combines the discounted spending on installations with the expected penalty for shortfalls. Value every installation at the cost of the component installed times the number of units, discounted to the period in which it occurs, and sum this over all components and periods. Then average the recorded shortfalls across the scenarios and charge the per-unit shortage penalty on that average. Set the total cost equal to the sum of these two amounts.

query: Suppose we cap lifetime reliance on C008 — no more than 10 units of it across the whole horizon. What does the optimizer return?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_c008 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x['C008', t] for t in model.t) <= 10)

query: We end up leaning on C008 about twenty times total. Wouldn't insisting on at least 22 of them give more headroom against the worst-case scenarios? What's keeping the optimizer from going that heavy?
expected_pyomo: model.why_c008_heavy = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x['C008', t] for t in model.t) >= 22)
